title: Initialization, Indeterminate Values, and Undefined Behavior · Memory Repair Bay
summary: An incident responder inherits a corrupted defined-state initialization audit, the earliest failing input, and two plausible hypotheses; they must preserve first-failure evidence, repair the cause, and add a regression. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in defined-state initialization audit and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Initialize every read-reachable scalar, member, padding-sensitive operation, and error-path state before use.
Avoid evaluating indeterminate values and understand when unsigned-char inspection is and is not permitted.
Explain how undefined behavior removes semantic constraints and invalidates debug-build intuition.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Initialization State Machine
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug native-systems team handles memory case 1655. Produce bounded C evidence to read only fields proven initialized by state; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Initialize every read-reachable scalar, member, padding-sensitive operation, and error-path state before use.
